input: We should ship the MVP next week and keep the scope tight. Emphasis on tight.
ideal: We should ship the MVP next week and keep the scope TIGHT.

input: The whole point of this refactor is maintainability not performance. Put emphasis on maintainability.
ideal: The whole point of this refactor is MAINTAINABILITY not performance.

input: Our onboarding needs to be simple and fast. Stress simple.
ideal: Our onboarding needs to be SIMPLE and fast.

input: This quarter is all about reducing churn. With emphasis on reducing churn.
ideal: This quarter is all about REDUCING CHURN.

input: The presentation should cover both cost savings and reliability. Emphasis on cost savings.
ideal: The presentation should cover both COST SAVINGS and reliability.

input: We need buy-in from engineering and from leadership. Stress leadership.
ideal: We need buy-in from engineering and from LEADERSHIP.

input: The thing users complain about most is load time. Emphasize load time.
ideal: The thing users complain about most is **load time**.

input: Make sure the error message is clear and actionable. Emphasize actionable.
ideal: Make sure the error message is clear and **actionable**.

input: Our differentiator is the depth of integration with existing tools. Emphasize depth of integration.
ideal: Our differentiator is the **depth of integration** with existing tools.

input: Well I think the real issue is that we never defined success criteria upfront.
ideal: Well, I think the real issue is that we never defined success criteria upfront.

input: I mean the latency numbers are fine but the p99 tail is what's killing us in production.
ideal: I mean, the latency numbers are fine but the p99 tail is what's killing us in production.

input: You know the tricky part about microservices is that failure modes are really hard to reason about.
ideal: You know, the tricky part about microservices is that failure modes are really hard to reason about.
